lines: Okay tonight it's the one about Hilda, the girl with the blue hair, remember her?
The one who lives in the wilderness with her mom and her little deerfox?
Yeah the deerfox is named Twig. He's like a fox but he's got these tiny little antlers.
And then what?
So Hilda's out drawing the mountains and a giant wakes up, a big lonely giant.
But then they gotta move into the city, into Trolberg, because of the trolls.
Why are the trolls bad?
They're not all bad, baby, they just come down from the mountains at night.
There's a big wall around Trolberg with bells, and the bells warn everybody.
And Hilda makes friends, David and Frida, and they join the Sparrow Scouts.
Is that like real scouts?
Sort of, with badges and everything, but they meet weird woodmen and elves too.
There's a tiny elf, a whole invisible elf kingdom, you gotta sign paperwork to even see them.
That's silly.
It is silly. Now lie back down, Twig the deerfox is already asleep.
Can we see the nisse next time? The one who lives in the empty space?
Yes, the nisse, next time. Eyes closed now.
stories.tell_note: Obvious: franchise heroine 'Hilda' and 'Trolberg' named aloud, plus Twig the deerfox, Sparrow Scouts, and the nisse — multiple clean canonical tells.

lines: So there's this big old stone abbey out in the woods and all the mice live there.
The mice live in a church?
Sort of an abbey, yeah, big great hall, and they have these huge feasts, cordial and pies and all that.
And there's a young mouse, kind of clumsy, his name's Mathias.
He wants to be brave like the old warrior mouse from the painting on the wall.
Then what happens?
Then this evil rat shows up, a one-eyed rat, Clooney the Scourge they call him, with a whole army, rats and weasels and stoats.
And they want the abbey?
They want the abbey and they want to eat up all the food.
So Mathias goes off looking for the lost sword, the great warrior's sword.
Is the sword magic?
Not magic, no, just real old and real sharp, hidden way up high where a sparrow king lives.
And the badgers and the otters and the hares help defend the walls.
The hares talk funny, 'jolly good, wot wot,' all that.
Drink your milk, c'mon, before it gets warm.
So the clumsy little mouse turns into a real warrior, the rats get beaten, and everybody feasts again.
I like the feast part.
Everybody likes the feast part. Okay. Sleep.
stories.tell_note: Medium: franchise never named aloud; 'Mathias' and 'Clooney the Scourge' are mangled canonical names (Matthias / Cluny), plus the mice abbey, vermin army, and lost warrior's sword pin it to Redwall.

lines: Want the one in the valley with the little round white fellow?
The one that looks like a hippo but isn't a hippo?
Right, him. So it's the very end of autumn and everybody's getting ready to sleep all winter.
Why do they sleep all winter?
Cause that's just what they do, they eat pine needles and curl up till spring.
But his friend in the green hat won't sleep, he's packing up his tent and his little mouth organ.
Where's he going?
South, on his own, like he does every year. Says he'll be back when the first warm wind comes.
And the little white fellow's sad his friend's leaving, Snuffkin, his name's Snuffkin.
So he gives him a present?
No, his friend just plays one last song down by the bridge and walks off whistling.
And the mamma, the one with the apron and the big handbag, she tucks everyone in.
Is the pappa there?
Yeah, the pappa's off writing his memoirs again, very important, he keeps saying.
Then it snows and snows and the whole valley goes quiet and white.
And he wakes up?
He wakes up one time too early, middle of winter, but that's a whole other night. Sleep now.
stories.tell_note: Subtle: franchise never named and the troll/valley names stay generic ('the little white fellow', 'the valley'); the lone tell is the mangled 'Snuffkin' (Snufkin), the green-hatted mouth-organ wanderer who heads south every winter — Moominvalley.

lines: Tonight it's the boy from the island, the one who's so good at magic he gets cocky.
Cocky means?
Means he shows off. The other young wizards at the school dare him to.
So he does this big spell he isn't ready for, tries to call up a spirit from the dead.
And then?
And this shadow thing tears out of the dark and claws his face and gets loose in the world.
And it chases him?
For years. Over the sea, island to island, in his little boat with the red sail.
Why doesn't he just hide?
Cause you can't hide from your own shadow, sweetheart. It's wearing his face.
His teacher told him everything's got a secret name, a true name, and if you know a thing's true name you have power over it.
Even a dragon?
Even a dragon. He met one, this old golden dragon out on the burnt islands, and he didn't even flinch. The dragon kept calling him by some other name, Spar-hawk, Sparrowhawk, something like that.
How does he beat the shadow?
Right at the very edge of the world, out on the open water, he turns around and faces it and says a name.
Whose name?
His own. The shadow had his name the whole time. Okay, that's enough now, eyes shut.
stories.tell_note: Subtle: world never named, hero is just 'the boy'; tells are the true-name magic, the island wizard school, the boat with the red sail, the shadow naming itself, and the mangled use-name 'Spar-hawk / Sparrowhawk' the dragon calls him — Earthsea/Ged.

lines: Okay, the young reporter, the one with the little tuft of hair and the white dog.
The dog that talks?
He doesn't really talk, he just thinks stuff and we hear it, his name's Snowy.
So the reporter and Snowy are chasing some crooks who stole something off a ship.
And the grumpy sailor guy helps?
Captain Hadock, yeah, the bearded captain, always shouting these funny insults.
Like what?
'Billions of blistering blue barnacles!' and 'thundering typhoons!', that kind of thing.
And he likes his whisky way too much, but never mind about that part.
Then the two detectives turn up, the ones in the matching bowler hats with the canes.
Tomson and Thomson?
Right, and they say everything twice and get it completely wrong every time.
What did the crooks steal?
A clue to a sunken treasure, some old scroll, so they all sail off to go find it.
Does the deaf professor come?
Professor Calculus with his little pendulum, yeah, can never hear a word anybody says.
Okay, settle down now, they find the treasure back home at the big manor. Goodnight.
Snowy too?
Snowy too. Lights off.
stories.tell_note: Medium: franchise not named and the hero is just 'the young reporter'; canonical tells are Snowy, the mangled 'Captain Hadock' (Haddock), the bowler-hat detectives (mangled 'Tomson and Thomson' for Thomson and Thompson), and Professor Calculus — clearly Tintin.
